title: The Hares And The Frogs
content:
Timid Hares, from the trumpeting wind,
    Fled as swift as the fear in their mind;
    Till in fright from their fear,
    From the green sedges near,
    Leaping Frogs left their terror behind.

    Our Own Are Not The Only Troubles
meaning:
Walter Crane's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Hares And The Frogs"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
